p.strong: Contrast: C vs Shell Truth Values

div.p: The shell inverts C's boolean convention
C Code: c int result = (5 > 3); // result = 1 (true) if (result) { ... } // executes
Shell: bash test 5 -gt 3 # exits 0 (true) if test 5 -gt 3; then ...; fi # executes
The if statement runs a command and checks its exit status. It does NOT evaluate a boolean expression!
if [ 5 -gt 3 ]; then ... ↑ This [ is the test COMMAND!
bash true # exits 0 if true; then ...; fi
bash false # exits 1 if false; then ...; fi
